title: Real-Time AI Voice & Streaming Assistant (Repo) — Scope
content: This repository is a production-minded reference implementation for real-time AI experiences: WebSockets for bi-directional sessions, SSE for simple server->client streams, and a lightweight RAG layer for grounding. It intentionally avoids heavy STT/TTS, queues, and microservices to keep the core streaming and observability concerns easy to reason about.

title: WebSockets vs SSE — Practical guidance
content: Use SSE when the client only needs a stream of server events and you want simple HTTP semantics and easy load balancing. Use WebSockets when you need bi-directional messages (interruptions, barge-in, backchannel, tool calls) or when you want a single long-lived session transport.

title: Observability strategy
content: Each request or stream is correlated by requestId + sessionId + traceId. Retrieval, generation, and streaming are instrumented with spans (duration logs) and Prometheus-style metrics (histograms + counters). Failure paths are intentional and explicit: timeouts, bad inputs, safety refusals, and provider fallbacks.

title: Guardrails and safety
content: This system includes basic input validation, rate limiting, and refusal behavior for unsafe requests. In production you'd replace the pattern-based safety layer with policy + model-based classifiers, plus strong authentication and per-tenant isolation.
